items.title: Prized WA tourism hotspots forced to close to holiday-makers for Easter

children.content: Famed holiday hotspots in Western Australia's north, including Exmouth and Coral Bay, are reluctantly turning away tourists ahead of the Easter holiday peak as they rebuild in the wake of Cyclone Narelle.

gallery.altText: An aerial shot of a bright blue rugged coastline

thumbnail.altText: An aerial shot of a bright blue rugged coastline

items.title: Man accused of failed Invasion Day rally bomb may enter insanity plea

children.content: A man accused of tossing a homemade "fragment bomb" filled with ball bearings and screws into a crowd in Perth's CBD flags through his lawyer that he may argue he is not criminally responsible for his actions due to insanity.

image.altText: Police speak to a man wearing a black butterfly print T-shirt, baseball cap and face mask

items.title: Man jailed for life for fatal stabbing of 'kind and loving' woman

children.content: Samuel Jade Lincoln Jingle will have to serve a minimum of 19 years in prison after killing his partner in Fitzroy Crossing.

image.altText: Exterior shot of the WA Supreme Court building in Perth with red kangaroo paws in the foreground at left.

items.title: Greyhound racing regulator defends controversial sport at inquiry

children.content: The body governing greyhound racing in WA says it is determined to maintain the welfare of both the dogs and the industry at a parliamentary inquiry, which was sparked by a petition that garnered 26,000 signatures.

items.title: 'Always assumed fuel would be available': Miner scales back as crisis bites

children.content: Junior West Australian iron ore miner Fenix Resources is forced to scale back non-essential activities to keep its operations running after coming within days of running out of fuel.

image.altText: Mining boss speaking at conference

items.title: Damage to pastoral stations a 'kick in the guts' after cyclone

children.content: West Australian pastoralists have faced severe flooding after ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle wreaked havoc in one of the state's most important food-producing regions.

image.altText: An aerial view of flooded cattle station

items.title: How one man's stolen identity was used to run a cult

children.content: In mid-2009 British citizen Simon Kadwill-Kelly was catching a ferry back from France. But as he tried to board, he learned his identity had been stolen — by the man accused of running an international cult from Nannup.

items.title: Twenty people winched to safety in WA floods after drivers ignore road closures

children.content: Authorities say motorists are repeatedly ignoring warnings not to drive in floodwaters in WA's north in the wake of Cyclone Narelle, with 20 people having to be rescued.

articles.title: Flooding leaves grieving boys stranded in Exmouth after father's death

children.content: Visitors in Exmouth who were stuck there when Cyclone Narelle passed are now trying to find ways out of the town, but that is proving impossible for one grieving family.

image.altText: A cardboard on a cafe bench with handwriting on it

articles.title: Apocalyptic scenes from Australia ahead of cyclone make worldwide news

children.content: Cyclone Narelle turned the sky over parts of Western Australia's north into an ominous red, sparking headlines across the globe.

image.altText: The streets of shark bay are cast in an eerie cloud of red due to TC Narelle

items.title: Fair Work Commission abolishes junior pay rates for young adults

children.content: The Fair Work Commission has issued its decision to abolish junior pay rates for young adult employees in the retail, fast food, and pharmacy sectors while maintaining them for minors.

image.altText: A person holds a turquoise coffee mug underneath the spout of an espresso machine

items.title: Transphobia nearly killed Kaz. Now he just wants to be 'me'

children.content: This WA teenager was depressed, ill and missing out on school. He says it was part of the toll transphobia took on him.

image.altText: A boy stands in front of ocean scenery.

items.title: Easter warning from WA police as South West road toll climbs

children.content: With 16 deaths on South West roads so far this year, police are pleading with holiday drivers to take a cautious approach.

image.altText: A blur of cars behind a 90km/h sign

items.title: Black-robed lawyers and 'killer' beef at Kimberley court

children.content: It was a court hearing unlike any other, complete with country music, "killer" beef, and gaggles of kids and dogs dashing between the black-clad lawyers.

image.altText: A man with a guitar surrounded by children

items.title: Freeman death stirs shock, sadness, anger in scarred Porepunkah

children.content: Seven months after a gunman killed two police officers on a nearby property, the tiny town woke to news Dezi Freeman was dead.

image.altText: A man in a dark jacket and pants holding a phone to his ear walks past a brick building that says "Punkah Pantry".

items.title: Telstra claims new coverage maps will 'make it much harder' for customers

children.content: ACMA has introduced new rules for standardised mobile coverage maps that will see mobile signals below -115 dBm labelled as "no coverage".

image.altText: The orange and white telstra sign is in focus in the background as people walk past in a blur.

items.title: 'I'm sick of it': One in two Australians priced out of healthcare system

children.content: One in two Australians missed out on health care they needed last year mainly because they could not afford it, according to a new report by the peak healthcare consumers group.

gallery.altText: A mum, a dad and their three children smile with forest behind them

thumbnail.altText: A mum, a dad and their three children smile with forest behind them

items.title: IOC's proposed gender test not fit for purpose, says man who discovered it

children.content: The scientist who discovered the SRY gene test that the International Olympic Committee is now relying on to determine athletes' eligibility for women's events says it should not be used in such a fashion.

image.altText: Olympic rings on a rainbow background.

items.title: Tim Rogers is an Aussie rock legend — that doesn't impress the bank

children.content: The frontman of ARIA Hall Of Famers You Am I on work, family, and why Hourly, Daily is still special to him.

image.altText: Tim Rogers wearing a dark pinstripe suit with an open patterned shirt, standing against a plain white background.

items.title: Exmouth beaches a 'graveyard' after ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle

children.content: In the popular tourist destination, it appears the category four cyclone has chewed up and spat out whole sections of the Ningaloo Reef onto dry land.

items.title: The 10 pivotal events that led to the downfall of Essendon and Carlton

children.content: Essendon and Carlton were once mainstays when it came to the business end of the AFL season. Over the last quarter century, both clubs have fallen on hard times. Here's why.

image.altText: Carlton and Essendon players line up for the national anthem

items.title: What happens if your house is underinsured?

children.content: If your home is underinsured, it means your insurance policy won't fully cover the cost of rebuilding, repairing or replacing it.

image.altText: Grandfather and grandson walking to a yellow weatherboard home across a green lawn

items.title: King penguin's arrival in WA prompts 'high-level' biosecurity response

children.content: A king penguin from the sub-Antarctic region is found more than 4,000 kilometres away from home on a remote beach near Esperance, on Western Australia's south coast.

image.altText: Sub-Antarctic king penguin found on WA's south coast in rare sighting

items.title: NASA begins countdown for humanity's first launch to the moon in 53 years

children.content: Four astronauts on the Artemis II rocket are set to blast off early Thursday morning.

image.altText: Four astronauts wearing blue jumpsuits standing in front of the tall Artemis II spacecraft on a clear day.

items.title: Supermarket chicken could get cheaper due to grain breakthrough

children.content: Australian researchers develop a high-protein sorghum variety, hoping to replace expensive livestock feed with systems that could lead to cheaper meat and eggs.

image.altText: UQ Professor Ian Godwin stands in a field of sorghum.

items.title: Five social media companies investigated over response to teen ban

children.content: Facebook, Instagram, Snapchat, TikTok and YouTube are being investigated for potential noncompliance with the ban, almost four months after the laws came into force.

image.altText: A red-haired woman in a light-coloured blazer stands in a corridor.

items.title: 'You are just an immigrant': Millions in unpaid wages, modern slavery uncovered

children.content: Millions in stolen wages, threats of visa cancellation and modern slavery by Australian employers have been uncovered in the first year of a national program.

items.title: Photos explore psychological drama of Australian rural life

children.content: Western Australian documentary photographer Brad Rimmer's beautiful, melancholy images of Wheatbelt life sprawl through Fremantle gallery.
